explanation: This 3-model ensemble achieves the highest holdout lower CI (0.0253) across all 108 combinations evaluated. The selection is based on several key factors: (1) Superior holdout performance - 38% improvement over best single model (0.0183) and 4% over best pair (0.0243), (2) Maximum diversity - combines DR-learner (doubly robust, best CV performer), T-learner LGB (tree-based), and X-learner (propensity-weighted, worst CV but adds complementary signal), (3) Equal weighting strategy proves optimal here, outperforming complex weighting schemes that likely overfit to validation patterns, (4) The ensemble balances model philosophies: DR-learner captures direct treatment effects robustly, T-learner LGB captures non-linearities, X-learner adds counter-factual reasoning especially valuable in heterogeneous treatment settings.
alternatives_considered: t_learner_lgb+r_learner+hurdle (0.0248): Second best triple, but R-learner and Hurdle are more correlated (both residual-based), reducing diversity benefit
t_learner_lgb+hurdle pair (0.0243): Best pair with gap_weights, but loses 10 basis points vs selected triple - the additional model diversity is worth it
dr_learner+hurdle pair (0.0240): Good performance but misses non-linear patterns that T-learner LGB and X-learner capture
Single dr_learner: While best CV performer (0.0118 lower CI), holdout is only 0.0183 - ensemble robustness clearly superior
Rank/gap weighted alternatives: Consistently underperform equal weights in top combinations, suggesting weighting schemes overfit to validation noise
overfitting_assessment: Low-to-moderate overfitting risk. The ensemble shows strong holdout performance (0.0253) despite including x_learner which had poor CV results (-0.0115 lower CI), indicating true complementarity rather than overfitting. The 38% improvement over single models on holdout data is substantial and unlikely to be noise. Equal weighting reduces overfitting compared to learned weights. The combination of methodologically distinct approaches (doubly-robust, tree-based, propensity-weighted) provides natural regularization through diversity. Key risk mitigation: all three models use different estimation principles, making correlated errors unlikely. The gap between best triple (0.0253) and second-best (0.0248) is modest (5 basis points), suggesting stable performance in this region rather than a lucky outlier.
